email_id.description: The email address of the team account

first_name.description: First name of the team account

last_name.description: Last name of the team account

invited_by.description: The ID of an existing team account. To get the id, please refer GET **/v2/Teams** endpoint

is_sso_user.description: Set this to true, if you are adding an SSO user

scheme_name.description: Specify to add the reader to the specific scheme name, if the reader is a Single Sign-On user. If the scheme name is not provided, then the reader will be added to the default scheme.

skip_sso_invitation_email.description:
Set this to true, if you would like to avoid sending an invitation email to the newly added team account.
This property is applicable only when adding a SSO user.

associated_portal_role_id.description: The associated portal role id. To get the list of portal roles refer /teams/roles endpoint.

associated_content_role_id.description: The content role id of the team account.Please refer GET **/Teams/roles** endpoint to get the list of content roles.

allOf.description: This is an enum. Possible values are 0 - None, 1 - Category, 2 - Version, 3 - Project, 4 - Lanaguage
allOf.title: AccessScopeLevel

access_level.description:
This is an enum. Possible values are
0 - None
1 - Category
2 - Version
3 - Project
4 - Lanaguage

project_version_id.description: The project version id to which the category belongs.

category_id.description: The category id to which the user should be given access.

language_code.description: The language to which the user should be given access.

categories.description:
A list of category scope objects.
This is only required if the access level is set 1 - Category

project_versions.description:
A list of project versions
This is only required if the access level is set to 2 - Version

project_version_id.description: The project version id to which the user should be given access.

language_code.description: The language to which the user should be given access.

languages.description:
A list of language scope objects
This is only required if the access level is set to 4 - Language

allOf.description: The access scope of the user
allOf.title: AddUpdateAccessScopeCustomerV2

access_scope.description: The access level of the team account. With the access level, you will be able to set the permissions at a granular level. For example, you can limit the user to view articles only for a particular language/category/version.

items.description: The content permission of the team account
items.title: ContentPermissionCustomer

content_permissions.description: The content level permissions for the newly added team account

associated_groups.description: A list of group ids to which the team account has to be added. Please refer /teams/groups endpoint to get the available groups.

allOf.description: Request to add a new team account user
allOf.title: AddTeamAccountCustomer

schema.description: Request to add a new team account user

Team Account - Add a new team account wi.summary: Here you can add new team account with content permission and access level as None.
Here you can add new team account with content permission and access level to Category.
Here you can add new team account with content permission and access level to Language.
Here you can add new team account with content permission and access level to Project.
Here you can add new team account with content permission and access level to Version.
